channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Asmah Majed rises as a leading female comedian in Tanzania
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, Asmah Majed, also known as Asmaah Jamida, is making big waves in Tanzania's comedy scene. Starting her journey in 2019 while still a university student and serving as student governm...

item.title: Nigeria to Host Intra-African Trade Fair 2027 in Lagos
item.description: Nigeria don officially sign host agreement for the fifth Intra-African Trade Fair (IATF2027), wey go hold for Lagos from 5th to 11th November 2027. Na big collaboration with African Export-Import Bank...

item.title: Old-growth forests in Sweden store significantly more carbon than managed woodlands
item.description: Most of Europe's original natural forests have been extensively altered for agriculture and timber production, leaving only small remnants of old-growth forests. In Sweden, boreal forests dominate, co...

item.title: Local and imported rice prices rise amid oil price hikes, says DA
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) reported that prices of local rice went up by as much as ₱3 per kilo compared to March 9, just before the major oil price hike took effect. Imported rice prices also...

item.title: Africa Check Debunks Fake Quotes Claiming Cult Leader Paul Mackenzie Linked President Ruto to Shakahola Deaths
item.description: Africa Check has dismissed social media graphics claiming that Paul Mackenzie, the self-proclaimed preacher at the center of the Shakahola forest tragedy, accused President William Ruto of being behin...

item.title: South Australian Election: One Nation's Rise and Preference Impact
item.description: The upcoming South Australian state election on Saturday presents a unique political landscape, particularly due to the surging popularity of One Nation. Historically, preference votes have been a key...

item.title: Iran Announces Eid al-Fitr Date Following Ramadan
item.description: Iran don announce say the last day of Ramadan go be Friday, and Eid al-Fitr, which be the big celebration marking the end of the fasting period, go start on Saturday. Dis announcement come directly fr...

item.title: Nigel Farage’s Cameo controversy highlights the commercialisation of his political persona
item.description: Gaby Hinsliff’s opinion piece in The Guardian examines Nigel Farage’s Cameo scandal, portraying it as further evidence of his transformation from a populist politician into a monetised public performe...

item.title: Survey shows more Filipinos now prioritize lower food prices amid inflation and oil hikes
item.description: A new Pulse Asia survey commissioned by Stratbase revealed that 41% of Filipinos want the government to prioritize making food prices more affordable. Conducted from February 27 to March 2, 2026, the ...

item.title: Canadian securities regulators launch pilot for semi-annual reporting for smaller issuers
item.description: Canadian securities regulators, under the Canadian Securities Administrators (CSA), have introduced a pilot project allowing smaller public companies to adopt a voluntary semi-annual financial reporti...

item.title: Israeli airstrike injures Russian journalists in Lebanon amid Middle East tensions
item.description: A Russian TV crew was injured during an Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon, highlighting escalating tensions in the Middle East. Russia Today's correspondent Steve Sweeney and cameraman Ali Rida we...

item.title: Pensioner in Vosloorus denied grant for months due to ID fraud
item.description: Sipho Mayo, a pensioner from Vosloorus, Ekurhuleni, only discovered in June 2025 that someone in Mamelodi, over 100km away, had been fraudulently collecting his old-age grant of R2,400 per month for 1...

item.title: Trump makes controversial Pearl Harbor remark during meeting with Japanese Prime Minister
item.description: During an Oval Office meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, U.S. President Donald Trump caused surprise by referencing the 1941 Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor while explaining his deci...

item.title: 15-year-old charged over air gun incident at Rose Bay Secondary College
item.description: A 15-year-old boy has been charged after allegedly firing an air gun at another student at Rose Bay Secondary College in Sydney's eastern suburbs. The incident occurred on Thursday afternoon around 1....

item.title: Global Terrorism Index 2026 Map Faintly Blurs Jammu and Kashmir with China
item.description: A map featured in the Global Terrorism Index 2026, which tracks terror activity in Pakistan and Afghanistan from 2007 to 2025, has sparked controversy by using faint and generalised borders. This make...

item.title: Chief Justice Earns Highest Judicial Salary in South Africa for 2025–2026
item.description: The Office of the Presidency has published an updated list of salaries for South Africa’s top judicial figures, effective from 1 April 2025. According to the gazette, the Chief Justice will earn over ...

item.title: Sam Amadi Praises Remi Tinubu’s Public Demeanor Amid UK Visit
item.description: During President Bola Tinubu’s official visit to the United Kingdom, former NERC chairman, Sam Amadi, shared his thoughts on the public conduct of Nigeria’s First Lady, Oluremi Tinubu. Amadi commended...

item.title: Taifa Stars announce 25-man squad for FIFA Series in Rwanda
item.description: Taifa Stars coach Miguel Gamondi has called up a 25-man squad for the upcoming FIFA Series matches in Rwanda, with camp starting on March 22. The squad is a mix of experienced internationals and promi...

item.title: Tensions Escalate in Gulf as Iran and US-Israel Conflict Impacts Energy Infrastructure
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have escalated after Iran retaliated against attacks on its South Pars gas field, targeting a US F-35 fighter jet and an Israeli oil refinery. Iran has warned of 'zero rest...

item.title: Three Jaish-e-Mohammad associates granted bail under GPS monitoring
item.description: In Jammu, the Jammu & Kashmir Police have equipped three alleged Jaish-e-Mohammad overground workers (OGWs) with GPS anklets following their release on interim bail as per a court order. The individua...
